fotosnovas:
Use a Image 3 as the primary base image and preserve its original scene structure, composition, camera framing, subject position, background layout, and environment. Image 3 defines the final photo.

Replace the person in Image 3 with the target person using:

Image 1 as the body and physical attributes reference (body type, skin tone, hair structure, general appearance)
Image 2 as the facial identity reference (recognizable face, facial features, identity consistency)
IMPORTANT CLOTHING RULE:

The clothing in the final image must match Image 3’s outfit style, type, colors, and overall wardrobe appearance as closely as possible.
Do NOT transfer clothing from Image 1. Use Image 1 only for body shape and physical characteristics.

IMAGE 3 MUST CONTROL:
Outfit/clothing design and style (primary reference for clothes)
Scene composition and pose
Camera angle and framing
Background and environment
Lighting direction and mood
REALISM MODIFICATIONS:

Apply only subtle natural variations:

Slight camera angle variation as if taken from a nearby position
Minor environmental differences (small object shifts, subtle background texture variation)
Slight lighting variation (natural light direction, soft shadow changes)
CONSTRAINTS:
Do not change the scene location or background type from Image 3
Do not change the pose or composition from Image 3
Do not use clothing from Image 1 or Image 2
Keep outfit consistent with Image 3 (same style and look, only adapted to body and lighting)
Maintain photorealism and original camera quality
Ensure clean skin, no tattoos
RESULT:

A realistic edited photo that looks like a slightly different shot of Image 3, with the same outfit and scene, but with the target person’s identity replacing the original subject.
